instruments.operation: uses `Georgetown-style MPAI` as a named project component with a traceable role in the public mechanism. Source context: "Applied Intelligence Mission Control." This is the handoff for a lossless study room designed specifically to host a graduate-level applied-intelligence program (five-course Georgetown-style MPAI schedule) with zero information loss across the learning chain. The canonical chain the room preserves is explicit: source work product — reading session — Cornell note — zettel / shared concept — flashcard — review state.

instruments.input: project source text and the named component `Distraction Capture`
instruments.operation: uses `Distraction Capture` as a named project component with a traceable role in the public mechanism. Source context: arm inflammation / typing strain (so dictation, quick capture, and low-friction conversion are first-class modes, not afterthoughts) and distractibility (so a dedicated Distraction Capture object exists with a return-to-task target). The room marries Obsidian-style per-course separation with cross-course Zettelkasten linking, wires to Flashcard Deluxe as the SRS endpoint, and uses Ebbinghaus-style spaced intervals.
